objectTypes.description: A named military operation conducted by an Army or joint force, the principal unit of work in the operations process.

properties.name: operationId
properties.description: Stable unique identifier for the operation.

properties.name: classificationMarking
properties.description: Classification marking for the operation record.

objectTypes.description: A plan or order produced by the operations process: OPLAN, OPORD, WARNORD, FRAGORD, or EXORD.
A mission statement assigning a task and purpose to a unit (who, what, when, where, why).
Commander's clear, concise statement of purpose, key tasks, and desired end state.
Description of the broad actions the force must take to achieve the desired end state.

properties.name: linesOfEffort
properties.description: Named lines of effort or operations.

objectTypes.description: A potential solution developed during MDMP COA development to accomplish the mission.
An event, area, or point in the battlespace where tactical decisions are required during execution.

properties.name: triggerCriteria
properties.description: Indicators or events that trigger this decision.

properties.name: associatedNAIs
properties.description: NAIs whose observation supports the decision.

properties.name: expectedDecisionTime
properties.description: Expected time the decision must be made.

objectTypes.description: A continuously-updated assessment by a staff section of the situation and the operation's progress.
Plan that establishes how the staff will assess the operation, including objectives, MOEs, MOPs, and indicators.
Criterion used to assess changes in system behavior, capability, or operational environment tied to measuring achievement of an end state, achievement of an objective, or creation of an effect.

properties.name: currentValue
properties.description: Latest observed value or qualitative state.

objectTypes.description: Criterion used to assess friendly actions tied to measuring task accomplishment.
Observable item of information used to inform a measure (MOE/MOP) or decision point.

properties.name: associatedMeasureId
properties.description: MOE or MOP this indicator informs.

properties.name: associatedNAIs
properties.description: NAIs from which this indicator is collected.

objectTypes.description: An assigned task associated with an operation, mission, or order.

properties.name: taskVerb
properties.description: Doctrinal task verb (e.g., seize, destroy, defeat).

properties.name: phaseId
properties.description: Phase in which the task occurs.

objectTypes.description: Means of regulating forces or warfighting functions in the operational area (e.g., boundaries, phase lines, FSCMs).

properties.name: name
properties.description: Name (e.g., PL BLUE, BCT North Boundary).

properties.name: controlMeasureType
properties.description: Doctrinal type (boundary, phase line, FSCM, OPAREA, etc.).

properties.name: effectiveTime
properties.description: When this control measure becomes effective.

objectTypes.description: A risk identified during planning or execution.
A supposition about the current or future situation, assumed to be true in the absence of facts.
A restriction placed on the command by a higher command (must do or cannot do).

properties.name: constraintKind
properties.description: Mandatory action ('must do') or restriction ('cannot do').

objectTypes.description: A planned phase of an operation with a distinct purpose and end state.
A recurring or scheduled event in the headquarters battle rhythm (board, bureau, cell, working group, OPT).
A staff section of a headquarters (G/S element).
A phase in the Joint Targeting Cycle.
Commander's targeting guidance establishing objectives, priorities, restrictions, and desired effects (JTC Phase 1 output).
A logical grouping of targets (e.g., enemy IADS, sustainment) associated with a target system.
A functional system in the adversary force or society, examined as part of target system analysis.
An entity or object considered for possible engagement or other action.
Working analytic record produced during JTC Phase 2 target development; contains target system analysis, identification, characterization, validation, and prioritization artifacts.
Nomination of a target for placement on a prioritized target list (e.g., JIPTL).

properties.name: justification
properties.description: Why the target should be engaged.

objectTypes.description: A desired or achieved effect on a target or system.
A clearly defined, decisive, and attainable goal toward which an operation is directed.
A requirement to collect information against a target, NAI, or TAI to support targeting or assessment.
Geographic area where information that will satisfy a specific information requirement can be collected.
Geographic area or point where the commander can engage with lethal or nonlethal effects to influence enemy actions.
Generic Army organization (any echelon) used as a parent type for units, formations, headquarters, and staff elements.
A unit headquarters (e.g., Corps HQ, Division HQ, BCT HQ).
A unit's headquarters where the commander and staff perform their activities (Main, Tactical, Mobile CP).

properties.name: cpType
properties.description: Main, Tactical, Mobile, Rear, Early Entry.

objectTypes.description: A specific tactical unit (battalion, company, platoon, squad).
A higher tactical formation (BCT, Division, Corps) treated as a single object for graph purposes.

properties.name: subtype
properties.description: Subtype (IBCT, SBCT, ABCT, light/heavy division, etc.).

objectTypes.description: A staff cell, working group, or board within a headquarters.
A joint force component, element, or staff cell external to the Army organization but operating in concert (e.g., JFE, JTCB, JTWG, JFACC).
Sustainment organization providing logistics, personnel services, or health service support (e.g., TSC, ESC, BSB, FSC, CSSB, BCT support battalion).

properties.name: subtype
properties.description: TSC, ESC, BSB, FSC, CSSB, etc.

objectTypes.description: A specific Army Supply Support Activity that performs receipt, storage, and issue of classes of supply.
An individual piece of equipment held by a unit, with serial number where applicable.
A model/type of equipment (e.g., M1A2 SEPv3, M109A6).
A weapon system (kinetic or non-kinetic) with engagement characteristics.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Type (small arms, indirect fire, missile, etc.).

objectTypes.description: An ISR sensor system (EO/IR, SAR, SIGINT, MASINT, etc.).

properties.name: phenomenology
properties.description: EO/IR, SAR, SIGINT, MASINT, RF, acoustic.

objectTypes.description: A vehicle/aircraft/vessel platform that hosts weapons, sensors, or comms (e.g., M1 Abrams, MQ-9, AH-64).

properties.name: subtype
properties.description: Wheeled, tracked, rotary-wing, fixed-wing, surface, subsurface.

objectTypes.description: A communications system (e.g., SATCOM terminal, JBC-P, Link-16 terminal).
A point-in-time maintenance status record for a piece of equipment.
Roll-up readiness report for an organization at a point in time.
A maintenance work order opened against a piece of equipment.
A military capability (functional ability), provided by equipment, units, or combinations thereof.
An Army class of supply (I-X).
A specific supply item identifiable by NSN/LIN.
A forecast supply requirement supporting a task, mission, or operation.
A submitted request for supply (e.g., SARSS request).
A specific point that issues supplies (e.g., ATHP, ROM, ASP, FARP).

properties.name: subtype
properties.description: ATHP, ROM, ASP, FARP, ROWPU, etc.

objectTypes.description: Authorized stockage list maintained by an SSA listing items, levels, and stockage.
Basic load of supplies a unit carries to commence operations (Class V, Class IX, etc.).
A logistics package built to deliver supplies to a unit during operations.
A node in the distribution network (port, airfield, railhead, MSR junction, supply point).

properties.name: nodeType
properties.description: Port, airfield, railhead, junction, supply point.

objectTypes.description: A transportation asset (truck, rail car, aircraft, watercraft) used to move supplies/personnel.
Operational area defined by a higher commander for a subordinate force, who has primary responsibility within it.
Area of concern to the commander, including those areas outside the AO that affect mission accomplishment.
Composite of conditions, circumstances, and influences (PMESII-PT) affecting the employment of capabilities.
A named geographic location relevant to operations (terrain feature, town, key infrastructure).
A designated route used for movement (MSR, ASR, air corridor).
